# Checkbox

Lets a user turn an independent option on or off.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Labels and hit area](#labels-and-hit-area)
- [Controlled values](#controlled-values)
- [Validation and accessibility](#validation-and-accessibility)
- [Choosing a control](#choosing-a-control)

## Installation

Add the Checkbox component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/checkbox
```

## Usage

Use a checkbox for an independent yes-or-no choice, or for each item in a group where any number of items may be selected. Bind `v-model` when application state needs the current value; omit it when ordinary native form submission is sufficient.

**Checkbox example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex items-center gap-2">
    <Checkbox id="airplane-mode" />
    <Label for="airplane-mode">Airplane mode</Label>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Checkbox,
} from '@/Components/Checkbox'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
</script>
```

## Labels and hit area

Give every checkbox a visible label. Match the label's `for` attribute to the checkbox `id` so selecting the text also toggles the control. Keep the wording affirmative and specific, such as “Send weekly reports,” rather than “Do not disable reports.”

**With label example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex items-center gap-2">
    <Checkbox id="terms" />
    <Label for="terms">Accept terms and conditions</Label>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Checkbox } from '@/Components/Checkbox'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
</script>
```

### Generated label association

`CheckboxControl` is a convenience wrapper that generates an id, associates its label, and forwards `v-model`, `value`, and `indeterminate`. Use the separate `Checkbox` and `Label` components when the layout needs description or error content.

**Checkbox control example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-2">
    <CheckboxControl v-model="checked">
      Airplane mode
    </CheckboxControl>
    <code class="text-sm">{{ checked ? 'checked' : 'not checked' }}</code>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { CheckboxControl } from '@/Components/Checkbox'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const checked = ref(false)
</script>
```

## Controlled values

A single checkbox normally binds to a boolean. When several checkboxes share an array model, give each one a distinct `value`; Vue adds and removes those values using native checkbox semantics. Do not use one shared boolean for a multi-item group.

```vue
<Checkbox v-model="channels" value="email" id="email" />
<Checkbox v-model="channels" value="sms" id="sms" />

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Checkbox } from '@/Components/Checkbox'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const channels = ref<string[]>(['email'])
</script>
```

### Indeterminate state

Set `indeterminate` when a parent checkbox represents a partially selected set. It is a visual state, not a third submitted value: keep the actual checked model in sync and clear the mixed state when the user makes an explicit choice.

```vue
<Checkbox
  v-model="allSelected"
  :indeterminate="someSelected && !allSelected"
  aria-label="Select all rows"
/>
```

## Validation and accessibility

Use `required` only when the user truly must opt in, and do not preselect consent. For validation, place the checkbox in a `Field`, set `data-invalid` on that field and `aria-invalid` on the checkbox, then render the explanation with `FieldError`. Group related checkboxes in a semantic `FieldSet` with a `FieldLegend`.

## Choosing a control

> **Use Radio Group for one choice: [Radio Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/radio-group)**
>
> When users must choose exactly one option from a visible set, use a Radio Group instead of making several checkboxes mutually exclusive.

> **Use Switch for an immediate setting: [Switch](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/switch)**
>
> Use Switch when toggling the control applies a setting immediately. Prefer Checkbox when the choice is submitted with the rest of a form.

Hosted documentation: [Checkbox](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/checkbox)
